stories.title: Dybantsa vs. Peterson — No. 1 vs. No. 2 Finally Meet Tonight in Las Vegas

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
No. 1 vs. No. 2. Tonight. 🏀

AJ Dybantsa makes his Vegas Summer League debut against Darryn Peterson. 9 PM ET on ESPN.

Peterson already has a 25/12 and a 28-point OT win on the books. The pressure is on.

#HoopHeroes #Wizards #Jazz

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Darryn Peterson: 25/12 debut. Then 28 points in OT. 🔥

Now Dybantsa has to answer. Tonight, Vegas, 9 PM ET on ESPN.

No. 2 is putting pressure on No. 1 before he even tips off.

#HoopHeroes #NBAummerLeague

facebook.long_form:
Tonight is the Summer League game everyone has been circling on the calendar.

AJ Dybantsa, the No. 1 overall pick by the Washington Wizards, makes his Las Vegas debut against the Utah Jazz at 9 PM ET on ESPN. Standing across the floor is Darryn Peterson, the No. 2 pick, who is already making Wizards fans nervous with what he's shown in Salt Lake City.

Peterson's resume through two Summer League games: a 25-point, 12-assist double-double against the Grizzlies, then 28 points and an overtime win against the Hawks. He shot 11 for 21 from the field and 4 for 7 from three. There are turnover concerns — 8 in his debut — but the upside is impossible to ignore.

Dybantsa averaged 25.5 points per game as a BYU freshman and was the leading scorer in Division I. He's built for volume creation at the rim and drawing fouls. Tonight is his first real answer to Peterson's early hype.

One more wildcard: Ace Bailey was listed out for the Jazz but the team reversed that Thursday morning and he's now available tonight. If Bailey plays, the Jazz have three legitimate rookie lottery picks on the floor against Washington in one Summer League game.

This is what Summer League is for. Be there at 9.
facebook.image_caption: No. 1 vs. No. 2, Las Vegas, tonight. Dybantsa vs. Peterson. The wait is over.

article.headline: Dybantsa vs. Peterson: The Summer League Showdown NBA Fans Have Been Waiting For

stories.title: LeBron Taking 76ers "Very Seriously" — Free Agency Race Gets More Complicated

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Shams: LeBron is taking the 76ers "very seriously." 👀

That's the strongest Philadelphia language since free agency opened.

Gansey connection. Maxey-Klutch bond. Jaylen Brown already there.

Windhorst still says Cavs lead. But this just got complicated. #HoopHeroes #NBA

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
LeBron to Philly would be WILD. 🤯

Maxey + Brown + LeBron in the East? That's not a team. That's a problem for everyone.

Draymond already opted out for this moment.

Where does LeBron land? #HoopHeroes #76ers

facebook.long_form:
This just got a lot more complicated.

ESPN's Shams Charania reported Thursday that LeBron James is taking the Philadelphia 76ers "very seriously" as a free agency destination. That's the strongest language any credible insider has used about Philadelphia since the moratorium lifted.

The 76ers have a real pitch. Team president Mike Gansey worked in the Cavaliers front office for years and knows LeBron's world. Tyrese Maxey is a Klutch client, the same agency that represents LeBron. And Philadelphia just added Jaylen Brown, giving the roster two capable perimeter weapons alongside a potential LeBron signing.

ESPN's Brian Windhorst still describes the Cavaliers as the front-runner, noting "the vibes point to Cleveland." Rich Paul, LeBron's agent, says a decision is not coming in the next few days. Mid-July is the working timeline.

Meanwhile, the chess pieces keep moving. Draymond Green opted out of his $27.7 million player option with the Warriors specifically to help Golden State create room for a LeBron pursuit. James Harden declined his $42.3 million Cavaliers option and is renegotiating a new deal, which keeps the Cavs' roster picture flexible for LeBron's potential return.

This is not a two-team race anymore. Four teams have a legitimate path to signing the greatest player of this generation. Who do you think it's going to be?
facebook.image_caption: LeBron. 76ers. "Very seriously." The free agency race just changed.

article.headline: LeBron Is Taking the 76ers 'Very Seriously' — The Free Agency Race Just Changed

stories.title: Giannis Picks No. 7 in Miami; $275M Extension Window Opens January 2027

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Giannis wore No. 34 for his ENTIRE career in Milwaukee. 🦌➡️🔥

In Miami? He's No. 7.

He won't sign his $275M extension until January 2027. But he's not going anywhere.

#HoopHeroes #Heat

facebook.long_form:
For 12 years in the NBA, Giannis Antetokounmpo wore one number: 34.

In Milwaukee, No. 34 became one of the most recognizable jerseys in basketball. Two MVP awards, a Defensive Player of the Year, a championship. Everything was built under that number.

Now it's No. 7.

The Miami Heat confirmed Thursday that Giannis has chosen No. 7 for his new chapter in South Beach. It's the first time in his professional career he's worn anything other than 34. The change is deliberate. He's committing to the identity of a new team.

On the contract side: Giannis is eligible to sign his extension as early as October 1, but per reports, he'll wait until January 6, 2027, which is the six-month mark from the trade date. That unlocks the full four-year, $275 million maximum. It's a formality. He's staying.

Paired with Bam Adebayo in the frontcourt, the Heat are building something the Eastern Conference hasn't seen before. A legitimate second big alongside arguably the most physically imposing player in the sport.

No. 7. New city. Same mission.
facebook.image_caption: No. 34 in Milwaukee. No. 7 in Miami. Giannis is all in.

article.headline: No. 34 in Milwaukee, No. 7 in Miami: Giannis Starts a New Chapter

stories.title: DeMar DeRozan Waived by Kings — Six-Time All-Star Now a Free Agent

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Sacramento Kings just waived DeMar DeRozan. 🚨

Six-time All-Star. 19.2 points per game last season.

He's 36, yes. But that midrange game doesn't age. Contenders — make the call.

#HoopHeroes #NBA #Kings

facebook.long_form:
The Sacramento Kings have waived DeMar DeRozan, and somewhere right now, a contender's front office is picking up the phone.

DeRozan, a six-time All-Star, averaged 19.2 points per game in 39 appearances last season before a hand injury cut his year short. When he was healthy, he was the same player he's been for the last decade: a midrange creator who draws fouls, plays within the offense, and provides scoring that teams with younger rosters often can't manufacture.

Sacramento is moving in a different direction. The Kings have young pieces to develop and cap flexibility to protect. DeRozan, who signed with Sacramento to stay competitive, ends up caught in the middle of a franchise shifting its timeline.

Now every contender with a roster spot and a need for reliable scoring should be in conversation. The Celtics just traded Jaylen Brown. The Heat are building around Giannis and Bam. The Cavaliers have LeBron's arrival pending. Any of those teams could use a proven, shot-creating veteran who doesn't need the ball to be dominant.

DeRozan won't cost a fortune on a short deal. Six-time All-Star production on or near a veteran minimum is not a small thing. This call gets made fast.
facebook.image_caption: Six-time All-Star. 19.2 points a game last season. DeMar DeRozan is now a free agent. Who's calling?

article.headline: DeMar DeRozan Waived by Kings — A Six-Time All-Star Is Now on the Market

stories.title: Paul George Goes PG13 in Boston — No. 13 Jersey Confirmed, Trade Bonus Waived

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
PG13 is back. 🍀

Paul George waived his $3.9M trade bonus and claimed No. 13 in Boston.

He left money on the table he didn't have to. That's not someone going through the motions.

#HoopHeroes #Celtics

facebook.long_form:
Paul George left $3.9 million on the table, and that might be the most important transaction Boston has made all week.

When a player waives a trade bonus, it's easy to gloss over. It's an accounting footnote. But in context, it says something: George isn't here for a check. He's here to win.

The Celtics confirmed Thursday that George has chosen No. 13 with Boston — the same number he wore in Indiana that made PG13 a household name. He switched to No. 8 in Philadelphia, but No. 13 is the George identity. The elite two-way wing. The player who gives everything on both ends when he's bought in.

His cap hit stays at $54.1 million. He holds a $56.6 million player option for 2027-28. Celtics general manager Danny Ainge explained the trade clearly: George dominated Boston in the playoff series last spring. The Celtics couldn't beat him, so they brought him in.

PG13 in green. No. 13 on the back. No bonus on the table.

That's a guy ready to work.
facebook.image_caption: No. 13 is back. PG13 waived his trade bonus and is fully locked in on Boston.

article.headline: PG13 Is Back — Paul George Waives Trade Bonus, Claims No. 13 in Boston
